participants.extracted: Adult patients with coronary artery disease and severe left ventricular dysfunction (left ventricular ejection fraction <30%) who were scheduled for off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting (OPCABG) at a single cardiac institute. Eligible patients included those with single-, double-, or triple-vessel disease and evidence of viability in the affected territory on myocardial perfusion scan. Patients undergoing urgent/emergent, congenital, valve, aortic, or combined surgery; those recently treated with levosimendan or other inotropes; and those with preoperative IABP, significant pulmonary, renal, or liver dysfunction, redo-CABG, or bundle-branch-block arrhythmias were excluded.

interventions.extracted: Patients were randomized to receive preoperative levosimendan infusion or placebo before off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting (OPCABG). The levosimendan arm received 200 μg/kg of levosimendan diluted in 50 mL normal saline and infused at 2 mL/h for 24 hours, starting 24 hours before surgery, without a bolus dose. The control arm received an identical-appearing placebo infusion (multivitamin diluted in normal saline) for the same duration and at the same rate. All patients otherwise received standard anesthetic and surgical management, with perioperative hemodynamic monitoring and rescue inotropic/mechanical support as needed.

outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure perioperative hemodynamic and clinical outcomes in adult patients with low left ventricular ejection fraction undergoing off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting (OPCABG) after preoperative levosimendan versus placebo. Hemodynamic outcomes included heart rate, arterial blood pressure, cardiac output/cardiac index, pulmonary capillary wedge pressure, central venous pressure, urine output, capnography, nasal temperature, and arterial blood gases, assessed at baseline before infusion (T0), during the first hour of infusion at 0, 15, 30, 45, and 60 minutes, 24 hours after drug administration before induction in the operating theatre (T1), intraoperatively as mean values every 30 minutes (T2), immediately after surgery on ICU admission (T3), and at 12, 24, and 48 hours postoperatively (T4, T5, T6). Adverse clinical outcomes planned for assessment were need for high inotropic support (defined as dobutamine >5 μg/kg/min and/or adrenaline >0.1 μg/kg/min and/or noradrenaline >0.1 μg/kg/min), low cardiac output syndrome (defined as cardiac index 2.2 L/min/m2 with pulmonary capillary wedge pressure >16 mmHg and PaO2 <60 mmHg), acute renal failure (serum creatinine increase >50% from baseline with or without oliguria or dialysis requirement), cerebrovascular accident/transient neurologic deficit (new focal neurologic deficit or coma persisting >48 h after exclusion of metabolic causes; <48 h considered TIA), postoperative mortality (death during hospitalization or within 30 days), atrial fibrillation, ventricular ectopics, need for intraaortic balloon pump, need for cardiopulmonary bypass conversion, ICU length of stay, hospital length of stay, and ventilator time; these were evaluated perioperatively and during the postoperative hospitalization (up to 30 days for mortality).
